promptLines: Read and choose the correct order of the paragraphs.
(A) What do plants need to grow? If you said light, soil, and water, you're right! But some plants have
different needs. They eat meat! Meat-eating plants usually grow in areas with poor soil. This soil can't
give them enough nutrients, so they've found another way to get it: eating insects and other small
creatures. Don't worry, though. Plants only eat people in books and movies!
(B) Another meat-eating plant is the pitcher plant. There are several varieties of the pitcher plant, and some

meter high. They can hold up to two liters of sweet, sticky liquid. Curious insects go inside for a drink,

questions.prompt: What do turtle sanctuaries around the world do? They help turtles __________ __________ and __________ their eggs. Around the world, people have opened turtle sanctuaries. They are protected areas that help turtles stay alive and protect their eggs. One of these sanctuaries can be found in Bora Bora. In 2000, a tourist at the hotel Le Mé ridien brought a wounded turtle to the hotel. After that, the hotel opened a turtle protection center. It works to help turtles protect their babies, treat inju
promptLines: What do turtle sanctuaries around the world do?
They help turtles __________ __________ and __________ their eggs.
Around the world, people have opened turtle sanctuaries. They are protected areas that help
turtles stay alive and protect their eggs. One of these sanctuaries can be found in Bora Bora. In
2000, a tourist at the hotel Le Mé ridien brought a wounded turtle to the hotel. After that, the hotel
opened a turtle protection center. It works to help turtles protect their babies, treat injured turtles,
and teach hotel visitors about the danger turtles are in.

promptLines: How does plastic affect seabirds?

promptLines: Read and choose the correct order of the paragraphs.

six million tourists in 2016, the most it's ever seen.

promptLines: What is the best word for the blank?
Interviewer: Has the village always been like this?
Lotte: No, it hasn't. The village's original inhabitants were miners, but the village was abandoned in
the 1960s when life became too difficult. In 1989, the town was re-inhabited by five friends from
Germany who had been searching for a place where they could live a self-sufficient lifestyle.
They were joined by an international mix of others who shared the same vision. The group
began to rebuild the existing houses and even built new ones. The eco-village that exists today
has about 60 inhabitants.

promptLines: What does the underlined word “them” refer to?
The team estimates that they have found evidence of up to 100 previously unknown standing stones.
However, they believe that there may be even more waiting to be discovered. Thirty or forty of them
are still buried beneath the surface. The so-called “super‑henge” is about two miles (3 km) away from

questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: 다음 문장의 형식을 더한 수로 올바른 것을 고르시오. (예시) 1형식 + 2형식 + 3형식 + 3형식 + 4형식 = 13 (1) She showed me her cat. ( 형식) (2) The Sun rises in the East. ( 형식) (3) I saw her crossing the street. ( 형식) (4) The doctor advised Ken to exercise regularly. ( 형식) (5) The baby's smile looks cute. ( 형식)
promptLines: 다음 문장의 형식을 더한 수로 올바른 것을 고르시오.
(예시) 1형식 + 2형식 + 3형식 + 3형식 + 4형식 = 13
(1) She showed me her cat. ( 형식)
(2) The Sun rises in the East. ( 형식)
(3) I saw her crossing the street. ( 형식)
(4) The doctor advised Ken to exercise regularly. ( 형식)
(5) The baby's smile looks cute. ( 형식)

questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: 다음 문장의 형식을 더한 수로 올바른 것을 고르시오. (1) The milk on the table went bad. (2) I will be a famous actor someday. (3) Nick reads the comics in the newspaper every morning. (4) Something smells bad in this room.
promptLines: 다음 문장의 형식을 더한 수로 올바른 것을 고르시오.
(1) The milk on the table went bad.
(2) I will be a famous actor someday.
(3) Nick reads the comics in the newspaper every morning.
(4) Something smells bad in this room.

questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: 밑줄 친 ①~⑤ 중에서 어법상으로 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오. Isaac Newton was a famous scientist. He ① lived in England a long time ago. One day, he ② was sitting under an apple tree. He saw an apple ② to fall to the ground. He found gravity*. Gravity pulls everything down to the ground. Since that day, his science ④has helped many people. Today, we ⑤ study his science in school. *gravity : 중력
promptLines: 밑줄 친 ①~⑤ 중에서 어법상으로 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오.
Isaac Newton was a famous scientist. He ① lived in England a long time ago. One day, he ② was
sitting under an apple tree. He saw an apple ② to fall to the ground. He found gravity*. Gravity
pulls everything down to the ground. Since that day, his science ④has helped many people.
Today, we ⑤ study his science in school. *gravity : 중력

questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: 다음 대화의 내용으로 올바르지 않은 것을 고르시오. Emily : Hi Tom, what are you writing on your note? Tom : I'm practicing my Chinese characters. I have a writing test tomorrow. Emily : Wow, that looks so difficult. Have you ever been to China before? Tom : No, I haven't. I've only studied Chinese at school. Have you learned any languages? Emily : Actually, I can speak English, Spanish and French. My mom is a Spanish teacher, and I used to live in France when I was young.
promptLines: 다음 대화의 내용으로 올바르지 않은 것을 고르시오.
Emily : Hi Tom, what are you writing on your note?
Tom : I'm practicing my Chinese characters. I have a writing test tomorrow.
Emily : Wow, that looks so difficult. Have you ever been to China before?
Tom : No, I haven't. I've only studied Chinese at school. Have you learned any languages?
Emily : Actually, I can speak English, Spanish and French. My mom is a Spanish teacher, and I
used to live in France when I was young.

Harry : I'm writing a report about politics*, and I was wondering if you would mind checking it. I'm a bit worried that it might be too hard to read. questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: 다음 대화문을 읽고, (A)~(C)에 들어갈 말로 적절히 짝지어진 것을 고르시오. Min-su: I'm so tired today. I might have a double shot Americano. Ji-won: But you should not drink too much caffeine. You won't (A)_______ sleep tonight. Min-su: You're right. Then, what should I get? Ji-won: Look! This Strawberry Latte looks great. It (B) be the most popular drink here. Min-su: That could be a good choice. What about you? Ji-won: I (C) like to try something warm. It must be very cold outside. Staff: Hello! May I take your order?
promptLines: 다음 대화문을 읽고, (A)~(C)에 들어갈 말로 적절히 짝지어진 것을 고르시오.

Ji-won: But you should not drink too much caffeine. You won't (A)_______ sleep tonight.
Min-su: You're right. Then, what should I get?
Ji-won: Look! This Strawberry Latte looks great. It (B) be the most popular drink here.
Min-su: That could be a good choice. What about you?
Ji-won: I (C) like to try something warm. It must be very cold outside.
Staff: Hello! May I take your order?

questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: 다음은 한 병원에서의 대화이다. 대화의 내용으로 적절하지 않은 것은? Jannet: Hello! How may I help you today? Client: Hi, I have an appointment* with Dr. James. Could I see him now? Jannet: Oh, I'm sorry. Dr. James has gone to Korea for a business trip* this week. He might be back in the office next Monday. Client: I see. Would it be possible to see him then? Jannet: Let me check... Monday morning is very busy. You could see him on Tuesday afternoon instead. Would that be okay?
promptLines: 다음은 한 병원에서의 대화이다. 대화의 내용으로 적절하지 않은 것은?
Jannet: Hello! How may I help you today?
Client: Hi, I have an appointment* with Dr. James. Could I see him now?
Jannet: Oh, I'm sorry. Dr. James has gone to Korea for a business trip* this week.
He might be back in the office next Monday.
Client: I see. Would it be possible to see him then?
Jannet: Let me check... Monday morning is very busy. You could see him on Tuesday afternoon
instead. Would that be okay?

questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: 밑줄 친 ①~⑤ 중에서 어법상으로 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오. The old garden remained ① quiet in the early morning. The colorful flowers smelled ② sweet in the cool air. A gentle breeze made the grass ③dancing softly. I kept my eyes closed and felt the soft wind ④touching my hair. I found the peaceful moment ④ perfect. Everything seemed magical in the golden sunlight.
promptLines: 밑줄 친 ①~⑤ 중에서 어법상으로 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오.
The old garden remained ① quiet in the early morning. The colorful flowers smelled ② sweet in
the cool air. A gentle breeze made the grass ③dancing softly. I kept my eyes closed and felt the
soft wind ④touching my hair. I found the peaceful moment ④ perfect. Everything seemed magical
in the golden sunlight.

questions.sectionTitle: 기본 문항
questions.prompt: ~⑤번 문장 중에서 어법상으로 옳지 않은 부분이 있는 것을 고르시오. Jun-ho wanted to make Min-ji's birthday special. He knew she loved 'Dubai Chewy Coockie', but it was very hard to find. He visited five different bakeries, and finally, ①he could buy Min-ji the last cookie. ②Next, he went home and wrote her a sweet letter. ③ He also made a small gift box with pretty ribbons to her. However, he wasn't sure about the meeting time, ③ so he asked a favor of his sister to check Min-ji's schedule. Finally, everything was ready. ④
promptLines: ~⑤번 문장 중에서 어법상으로 옳지 않은 부분이 있는 것을 고르시오.
Jun-ho wanted to make Min-ji's birthday special. He knew she loved 'Dubai Chewy Coockie', but
it was very hard to find. He visited five different bakeries, and finally, ①he could buy Min-ji the
last cookie. ②Next, he went home and wrote her a sweet letter. ③ He also made a small gift box
with pretty ribbons to her. However, he wasn't sure about the meeting time, ③ so he asked a
favor of his sister to check Min-ji's schedule. Finally, everything was ready. ④ On her birthday,
Jun-ho gave Min-ji the gift with a big smile. ⑤ Min-ji looked very happy.

questions.sectionTitle: 전체

questions.prompt: 다음 우리말과 같도록 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? [5점] [접속사] 떠나라, 그러면 너는 자유를 찾을 수 있을 거야. → Leave, __________ you will find freedom. (14 ~ 14) ( ) 안의 말을 이용하여 문장을 다시 쓰시오.

questions.answer: Put on your coat, or you'll catch a cold.
questions.sectionTitle: 전체
questions.prompt: 우리말과 일치하도록 주어진 조건에 맞게 문장을 완성하시오. [5점] [접속사] 조건 1 명령문을 사용할 것 조건 2 ( ) 안의 말을 포함하여 총 9단어로 쓸 것 너의 코트를 입어라, 그러지 않으면 너는 감기에 걸릴 것이다. (put on, catch a cold)
promptLines: 우리말과 일치하도록 주어진 조건에 맞게 문장을 완성하시오.
[5점] [접속사]
조건 1 명령문을 사용할 것
조건 2 ( ) 안의 말을 포함하여 총 9단어로 쓸 것
너의 코트를 입어라, 그러지 않으면 너는 감기에 걸릴 것이다.
(put on, catch a cold)
